title: 10 best herbal teas for grey hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas for grey hair are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are believed to help maintain natural hair color and prevent premature graying. These teas typically contain high levels of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth. Examples of herbal teas that are commonly used for gray hair include rooibos, sage, rosemary, and nettle. By consuming these teas regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in the appearance of gray hairs and an improvement in the overall health and shine of their hair. This can lead to increased confidence and self-esteem, as well as a decreased need for harsh chemical treatments that can damage the hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying. The tea also contains vitamin C, which is essential for the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. Additionally, lemon verbena has anti-inflammatory properties that can help improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially delaying the onset of gray hair. Drinking lemon verbena tea or using it as a rinse may help maintain the natural color of your hair and keep it looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as verbascoside and rosmarinic acid, which help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the likelihood of graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, like luteolin and apigenin, that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially slowing down the graying process.

Melanin synthesis support: Lemon verbena tea contains active constituents, including caffeic acid and rutin, that support melanin synthesis, the pigment responsible for hair color, helping maintain natural hair color.

Hair growth promotion: Lemon verbena tea stimulates hair growth by prolonging the anagen phase, the growth stage of the hair cycle, due to the presence of ursolic acid and other polyphenols, resulting in healthier and potentially slower graying hair.

DHT blockage: Lemon verbena tea has been found to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to hair graying and thinning. By blocking DHT, lemon verbena tea helps maintain hair color and thickness.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried lemon verbena leaves, bringing them to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10 minutes to allow the herb to release its nutrients.
Remove from heat, add 1 tablespoon of dried nettle leaves and let it steep for another 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used herbs.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and enjoy your freshly brewed herbal lemon verbena tea for grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and nutrients that can help to reverse graying hair. Brahmi, also known as Bacopa Monnieri, is an herb that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to promote healthy hair growth and reduce graying. The antioxidants in brahmi tea help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to graying. Additionally, brahmi contains essential fatty acids and vitamins that nourish the hair and support healthy hair growth, helping to keep your hair strong, thick, and vibrant. Regular consumption of brahmi tea may help to slow down the graying process and promote healthy, youthful-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi tea contains antioxidants that help protect hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying. The active constituents responsible for these antioxidant properties include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ascorbic acid and β-sitosterol, that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially slowing down the graying process.

Nutrient-rich properties: Brahmi tea is rich in vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients that nourish the hair and scalp, supporting healthy hair growth and pigmentation. Key nutrients include vitamin C, vitamin A, calcium, and magnesium.

Stress-relieving properties: Brahmi tea has adaptogenic properties that help the body cope with stress, which can contribute to premature graying. The active constituents responsible for these stress-relieving properties include bacosides and bacopasaponins, which help regulate stress hormones and promote relaxation.

Hair growth promotion: Brahmi tea stimulates hair growth and may help maintain natural hair color by improving blood circulation in the scalp and nourishing hair follicles. The active constituents responsible for these hair growth-promoting properties include sterols, alkaloids, and saponins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried brahmi leaves to the boiling water, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent brahmi leaves.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added flavor, then enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with grey hair because it contains high levels of antioxidants, flavonoids, and carotenoids that can help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals. These compounds can also help to stimulate the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. Additionally, pot marigold tea is rich in vitamin C, which is essential for the production of collagen, a protein that helps to keep hair strong and healthy. By promoting healthy hair growth and protecting the hair follicles, pot marigold tea can help to delay the onset of grey hair and keep hair looking vibrant and youthful.
remedy_properties: Anti-oxidant properties: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants such as flavonoids, which help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, preventing premature graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and luteolin, which can reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and slowing down the graying process.

Nutrient-rich: Pot marigold tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, B-complex vitamins, and potassium, which nourish the hair, support hair pigmentation, and maintain a healthy scalp.

Melanin stimulation: The tea contains compounds that may help stimulate melanin production, the pigment responsible for hair color. This can help maintain hair color and slow down the graying process.
Hair growth promotion: Pot marigold tea can promote hair growth by stimulating hair follicles and improving the health of the scalp, which can indirectly help prevent and reduce gray hair.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marigold flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the water to absorb the marigold's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the marigold flowers, leaving you with only the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, then let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1-2 cups of marigold tea daily for at least a month to see improvements in grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and anthocyanins, which can help to reverse the graying process by protecting hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals. The tea also helps to stimulate hair growth and improve circulation in the scalp, which can lead to healthier, stronger hair and potentially slow down the graying process. Additionally, cornflower tea contains essential nutrients like vitamin C and iron, which are important for maintaining hair health and preventing premature graying. Regular consumption of cornflower tea can help to promote overall hair health and reduce the appearance of gray hairs.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cornflower tea contains antioxidants, such as anthocyanins, that help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress, which can contribute to premature graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the scalp, promoting healthier hair growth and potentially slowing down the graying process.

Nutrient-rich: Cornflower tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, B-complex vitamins, and magnesium, which support overall hair health and may help maintain the natural color of hair.

Melanin synthesis support: Cornflower tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help stimulate melanin production, the pigment responsible for hair color, thereby potentially slowing down the appearance of gray hair.

Blood circulation enhancement: The tea's active constituents may help improve blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring that hair follicles receive essential nutrients and oxygen, promoting healthier hair growth and potentially reducing graying.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried cornflower petals.
Place the cornflower petals in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the petals and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the petals.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, as part of your hair care routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in nutrients that promote hair health, including silica, selenium, and cysteine. Silica, in particular, is known to strengthen hair and improve its luster, while selenium and cysteine can help to prevent hair loss and damage. Additionally, horsetail tea contains antioxidants that can help to protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the graying of hair. By nourishing the hair and protecting it from damage, horsetail tea can help to maintain the natural color of hair and prevent premature graying. However, it's important to note that while horsetail tea may help to promote hair health, it is not a guaranteed cure for gray hair.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the horsetail herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the herb.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, consistently, for several months to potentially see improvements in grey hair.
remedy_properties: High silica content: Horsetail tea is rich in silica, which is important for grey hair as it helps to strengthen hair and promote hair growth, making it less likely to break and turn grey.
Antioxidant properties: Horsetail tea has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect hair follicles from damage and reduce the appearance of grey hair.
Rich in minerals: Horsetail tea contains several minerals, including calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which are important for maintaining healthy hair and reducing the likelihood of grey hair.
Promotes blood circulation: Horsetail tea has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help to promote hair growth and reduce the appearance of grey hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation of the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the likelihood of grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and vital nutrients that promote hair health. The tea is known to stimulate the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. It helps to nourish the hair follicles, improve blood circulation, and prevent premature greying. Regular consumption of maidenhair tree tea can help maintain the natural color of hair and promote healthy hair growth. The tea's anti-aging properties also help to delay the appearance of grey hair, giving you younger-looking and more vibrant hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-oxidative property: Maidenhair tree tea contains antioxidants that help protect hair follicles from free radical damage, which can contribute to premature graying. Active constituents include gallic acid and catechins.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chronic inflammation can cause hair graying, and maidenhair tree tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce this risk. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoids and flavonoids.

Melanin synthesis support: Maidenhair tree tea promotes melanin synthesis, which is essential for maintaining natural hair color. The active constituent responsible for this property is 2,5-dimethyl-7-hydroxychroman.

Hair growth promotion: Maidenhair tree tea stimulates hair growth, which can help maintain a fuller head of hair and potentially delay the onset of graying. Active constituents responsible for this property include oleanolic acid and ursolic acid.

Nutrient-rich: Maidenhair tree tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that support overall hair health, including vitamin C, vitamin E, and various B-vitamins. These nutrients help maintain healthy hair and prevent premature graying.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-3 teaspoons of dried maidenhair tree leaves and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes to ensure it's not too hot and will not damage the herbal properties.
Pour the hot water over the dried leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the leaves.
Drink the tea once or twice a day, consistently, to potentially help with grey hair over time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sea buckthorn tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in vitamins and antioxidants that can help to reverse the graying process. The tea is particularly high in vitamin C and vitamin E, which are both essential for maintaining healthy hair. These vitamins help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to graying. Additionally, sea buckthorn tea contains B vitamins, which are important for the production of melanin, the pigment that gives hair its color. By drinking sea buckthorn tea regularly, you can help to nourish your hair, support melanin production, and potentially slow down or even reverse the graying process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sea buckthorn tea is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and vitamin C, which help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the likelihood of premature graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea contains compounds like phenolic acids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to maintain a healthy scalp and reducing inflammation that could contribute to graying.

Nutrient-rich composition: Sea buckthorn tea is packed with vitamins, minerals, and essential fatty acids that nourish hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of gray hairs.

Melanin synthesis support: Sea buckthorn tea contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, copper, and iron, that support the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color, helping to maintain natural hair color.

Hair growth promotion: The tea's high antioxidant content and essential fatty acids, such as omega-7, promote hair growth and help prevent hair loss, which can contribute to the appearance of gray hairs.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried sea buckthorn leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent leaves.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, once or twice a day, consistently for several months to notice improvements in grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tea, also known as hops tea, helps with grey hair because it contains a high amount of antioxidants and minerals that can help to reverse the graying process. The tea is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which can help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals and environmental factors. Additionally, hops tea contains melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color, which can help to restore the natural color of hair. Regular consumption of hops tea can also improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Humulus lupulus tea contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the likelihood of premature graying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea has anti-inflammatory compounds, like beta-acids and humulones, that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially slowing down the graying process.

Melanin synthesis support: Humulus lupulus tea contains active constituents, such as prenylflavonoids, that may support melanin synthesis, the pigment responsible for hair color, helping to maintain natural hair color and prevent graying.

Dihydrotestosterone: The tea contains compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, that may inhibit the conversion of testosterone to DHT, a hormone associated with hair graying and hair loss, promoting healthier hair growth and potentially reducing graying.

DNA protection: Humulus lupulus tea has DNA-protective properties due to its antioxidant and anti-mutagenic constituents, which can help protect hair follicle cells from damage and mutations that might contribute to premature graying.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus (hops) flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the hops flowers, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 1-2 cups of the tea daily, consistently, for best results in promoting natural hair color.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane tea helps with grey hair because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to protect the hair follicles and scalp from damage. The tea is also known to improve blood circulation, which can help to stimulate the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. Additionally, elecampane tea contains high levels of vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, B vitamins, and iron, which are essential for healthy hair growth and can help to prevent premature graying. Drinking elecampane tea regularly may help to maintain the natural color of your hair and promote overall hair health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elecampane tea helps reduce inflammation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially slowing down the graying process. This property is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Antioxidant: Elecampane tea contains antioxidants that help protect hair follicles from free radical damage, which can contribute to premature graying. The antioxidant properties come from flavonoids and phenolic acids found in the plant.

Anti-melanin depletion: Elecampane tea may help prevent melanin depletion in hair follicles, maintaining natural hair color. This is attributed to the presence of eudesmanolides, which have been shown to inhibit melanin degradation.

Circulation-enhancing: Elecampane tea improves blood circulation in the scalp, ensuring that nutrients reach hair follicles for optimal hair health and potentially slowing down the graying process. The circulation-enhancing effect is due to the essential oil content, including caryophyllene and humulene.

Anti-stress: Elecampane tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress-induced graying. The anti-stress properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anxiolytic effects.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried elecampane root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 teaspoon of dried sage and 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary.
Cover and let the tea steep for 10 minutes before straining.
Drink 1-2 cups daily, consistently for several months, to potentially see improvements in grey hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with grey hair because it contains high levels of antioxidants and nutrients that promote hair growth and prevent hair damage. One of the main active compounds in lavender, called linalyl acetate, has been shown to help generate the growth of cells and reduce stress, which can both contribute to premature graying. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory properties that can improve scalp health and support the production of melanin, the pigment responsible for hair color. By drinking English lavender tea regularly, you can provide your body with the essential nutrients it needs to maintain healthy, vibrant hair and potentially slow down the graying process.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender tea has high levels of antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which help protect hair follicles from oxidative stress, reducing graying caused by free radical damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, like linalyl acetate and linalool, may help soothe an irritated scalp and promote healthy hair growth, which can minimize the appearance of gray hairs.

Hormone-balancing properties: English lavender tea contains polyphenols and phytoestrogens that may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to premature graying by affecting melanin production in the hair follicles.

Stress-relieving properties: The soothing and calming effects of lavender tea, attributed to its essential oils, can help reduce stress and anxiety levels, which can indirectly slow down the graying process as stress is a known factor in premature graying.

Nutrient-rich properties: English lavender tea contains essential vitamins and minerals, such as A, C, and calcium, that nourish the hair and scalp, promoting overall hair health and potentially reducing the occurrence of gray hairs.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes to allow the nutrients to extract from the lavender.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey or lemon juice for taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, 1-2 times a day, consistently for several months to see potential improvements in grey hair.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat grey hair with herbal teas is by rinsing your hair with strong brews of herbal infusions. Chamomile tea brings out natural highlights and adds shine, while sage tea darkens hair and promotes growth. Rooibos tea, rich in antioxidants, helps maintain hair health and can slow down the graying process. Regular use of these herbal teas as a final rinse after washing your hair can result in softer, stronger, and more vibrant locks.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to grey hair that are treated with herbal teas include hair loss and damaged hair. For hair loss, teas made from nettle, rosemary, and ginkgo biloba are often recommended due to their high nutrient content and stimulating effects on the scalp. Damaged hair can be treated with tea rinses made from chamomile, sage, and rooibos, which are rich in antioxidants and help to smooth the hair cuticle, add shine, and promote hair growth. These herbal teas can be used as a rinse after shampooing or as a leave-in treatment, depending on the desired result. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can support hair health, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for serious hair conditions.
